chunks.preview: ## [§1.1.d] Scaling to Gym Launch (5,000+ locations, 4:1 first-30-day ROAS)  So I eventually took that multi-step money model and licensed everything about it to 5,000 plus locations. And that's what became Gym Launch. And the average return on ad spend in the first 30 days across all those gyms, all right, not including memberships to this day is four to on

## [§1.2.c] The three numbers that matter — CAC, GP, Payback Period  Which then leads us to the only three numbers that matters, and this is what you need to write down. This is what you need to think with. All right? Now, this is not in the book, and you're like, "Wait a second, how are you not talking about this in the book?" It's 'cause no one can do math

We talked about the three things for acquisition. So we've got cost to acquire a customer, how much it costs us; gross profit, what we make for what it costs us; and now, we go to payback period, which is how fast this all happens. So if you see PPD, just payback period or payback period. I'll probably just say it out loud 'cause I don't wanna confuse you. A
